---
title: "UIAO ConMon Corrective-Action Playbook"
subtitle: "45-day internal cure window for RFC-0026 CA-7 deliverables"
author: "UIAO Governance"
date: "2026-04-21"
categories: [conmon, fedramp, rfc-0026, ca-7, corrective-action]
---

## Purpose

FedRAMP RFC-0026 (discussion [#130](https://github.com/FedRAMP/community/discussions/130))
specifies a five-strike corrective-action ladder that begins at the point
the FedRAMP PMO detects a failure to meet a monthly CA-7 deliverable.
Strike 1 issues a notification, a Corrective Action Plan (CAP) request,
and a one-month grace period; strikes escalate through agency
notification, Marketplace status changes, revocation-pending, and
certification revocation + Marketplace removal.

This playbook layers a **45-day internal cure window** *in front of* the
PMO-issued strike clock, per **ADR-043 D5**. The internal cure is
self-imposed — it is not an RFC-0026 obligation — and exists solely so
UIAO catches its own drift before the PMO notification fires. It
operationalizes the comment filed by `lrSpriggs` on discussion #130 that
asked for an accelerated, single-opportunity cure window.

## Scope

Triggered by any of the following detected internally:

1. **RV5-CA07-VLN — missed monthly scan.** Any CA-7-tagged
   conformance adapter (`scubagear`, `vuln-scan`, `stig-compliance`,
   `patch-state`, `intune`) fails to produce `findings.json` for a
   scheduled calendar month.
2. **RV5-CA07-VLN — missed monthly POA&M.** The `conmon-aggregate`
   workflow fails to emit the POA&M CSV, or the CSV fails schema
   validation, or the Connect.gov upload is not completed before the
   calendar-month rollover.
3. **RV5-CA07-VLN — missed annual IA scan.** The 3PAO engagement
   anniversary lapses without a scheduled assessment.
4. **RV5-CA07-CCM — missed monthly meeting.** No monthly ConMon
   meeting is held, or the agenda / minutes / recording is not
   attached to the evidence pack for that month.
5. **72-hour critical-finding SLA breach.** Any `CRITICAL` finding
   (per ADR-043 D2 / UIAO_132 §2.2) has no acknowledged remediation
   plan within 72 hours of issue creation.

## The 45-day clock

Day `D+0` is the first business day on which the missed deliverable
could have been detected (for monthly items, the first business day
following the calendar-month rollover; for the 72-hour SLA, the SLA
breach timestamp).

| Day | Action | Owner |
|---|---|---|
| D+0 | Governance issue opened with label `conmon-cure` and the triggering evidence path | `conmon-aggregate` workflow (automatic) |
| D+1 | Root-cause analysis assigned; missing deliverable re-scheduled | conmon-steward |
| D+7 | Interim status post to the `conmon-cure` issue; if unresolved, escalate to governance-steward | conmon-steward |
| D+14 | Draft remediation merged; re-run of missed scan(s) begun | adapter owner |
| D+30 | Re-run complete; missed deliverable emitted; POA&M amended if required | conmon-steward |
| D+45 | Cure closeout; `conmon-cure` issue closed with evidence linking to the remediated month's artifact | conmon-steward |

If day D+45 passes with the deliverable still missing, the cure has
failed. At that point:

1. The `conmon-cure` issue is relabeled `conmon-cure-failed` and
   remains open.
2. A pre-emptive notification is sent to the FedRAMP PMO in advance
   of the first PMO-issued strike.
3. An ADR-043-superseding ADR is opened to reconsider the pathway
   posture (typically: does Pathway 2's manual cadence still fit, or
   does the failure argue for accelerating the Pathway 1 migration?).

## Governance issue template

```
Title: [conmon-cure] <deliverable> missed — <yyyy-mm>
Labels: conmon-cure, ca-7, rfc-0026
Body:
  Trigger: <scope item 1–5 above>
  Deliverable: <e.g., "scubagear monthly findings.json">
  Expected by: <yyyy-mm-dd>
  Detected at: <yyyy-mm-ddThh:mmZ>
  Evidence path (expected): <evidence/conformance/<adapter>/<run-id>/>
  Root cause (D+1): <fill in>
  Remediation plan (D+1): <fill in>
  Closeout evidence (D+45): <fill in when resolved>
```

## Relationship to the PMO strike ladder

The internal cure runs **in parallel with**, not instead of, the PMO
clock. If the PMO issues Strike 1 during the 45-day cure window, the
PMO's one-month grace period and CAP requirement take precedence; the
internal cure continues as a tracking wrapper. If the internal cure
closes before the PMO notices — the design target — no external strike
is ever issued.

## Cross-references

- **ADR-043 D5** — the decision that establishes this playbook.
- **UIAO_132 §2.2** — 72-hour critical-finding SLA (scope item 5).
- **UIAO_132 §7 O6** — the open item this playbook closes.
- **FedRAMP RFC-0026** — [FedRAMP/community discussion #130](https://github.com/FedRAMP/community/discussions/130).

## Change log

| Version | Date | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 0.1 | 2026-04-21 | Initial draft alongside ADR-043 / UIAO_132 |
